content.problemStatement:
Abdul always suffered from anxiety and fear. To understand him better, the ingenious Doctor used a complex method to calculate his fear. The doctor wants to automate the process and hence needs your help!



Given an array AAA of NNN integers you have to find the index of the first smaller element to the right of every index iii, let it be jjj, then the fear[i]fear[i]fear[i] of the index would be j+1−ij + 1 -ij+1−i, and if there is a no smaller element to the right of index iii then the fear[i]fear[i]fear[i] = 1, we have to return the product of all the fear values mod 109+710^9 + 7109+7 .


Input Format
The first line contains NNN,the next line contains the array of NNN integers



Output Format
Output a single integer denoting the total fear.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the product of all fear values modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:
If we find the fear of every index:
Index 555 : fear is 1
Index 444 : fear is 2
Index 333 : fear is 2
Index 222 : fear is 3
Index 111 : fear is 1
Therefore the total fear is 1∗2∗2∗3∗1=121*2*2*3*1 = 121∗2∗2∗3∗1=12

solution.approach: For each index we need the first smaller element to its right. This is the classic Next Smaller Element problem and can be solved in O(N) using a monotonic stack that stores indices of a strictly increasing sequence of values when scanning from right to left. While processing position i, we pop indices whose values are >= A[i] because they cannot be the first smaller for any earlier element. After the pops, if the stack is empty there is no smaller element to the right, so fear[i] = 1. Otherwise the top of the stack is the nearest smaller element at index j, and fear[i] = (j - i + 1). We multiply all fear values modulo MOD = 1e9+7. The algorithm runs in linear time and uses O(N) additional space for the stack.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, because each array element is pushed and popped at most once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the stack and O(1) extra variables.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys
MOD = 1_000_000_007

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        a = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        stack = []  # will store indices
        ans = 1
        for i in range(N-1, -1, -1):
            while stack and a[stack[-1]] >= a[i]:
                stack.pop()
            fear = 1
            if stack:
                fear = stack[-1] - i + 1
            ans = (ans * fear) % MOD
            stack.append(i)
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
